name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-3
title: Research: does SKILL.md reload mid-conversation or is it cached at session start?
description: When a SKILL.md changes during an active conversation, determine whether Claude Code reloads the skill on the next invocation or continues to use the content cached at session start. Steps: (1) Check Anthropic official documentation for any stated behavior around slash command / skill content loading lifetime. (2) Form a hypothesis based on doc findings and mechanical reasoning about how Claude Code injects skill content. (3) Design and run a concrete experiment: modify a SKILL.md mid-session and observe whether the changed content is reflected in the next skill invocation within the same session. Findings should drive a decision on whether .rabbit-skills-updated reload logic is meaningful within a single session or only effective at session boundaries.

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
research task filed 2026-05-15; reopening for follow-through per user audit 2026-05-18

Experiment confirmed: SKILL.md reloads on every invocation within the same session, not cached at session start. Steps: (1) opened Claude Code in exp/ with SKILL.md at VERSION: v1, invoked /some-skill → got v1; (2) edited SKILL.md to VERSION: v2 without restarting; (3) invoked /some-skill again → got v2 immediately. Conclusion: Claude Code reads SKILL.md fresh on each invocation. The Inv 24 message 'reload automatically on next invocation' is accurate. No spec or implementation changes required.
